projects.title: Thrive
projects.subtitle: Plant Doctor App
projects.description: A mobile app that diagnoses plant diseases via real-time scans and provides comprehensive care tracking.
projects.problemStatement: Many plant owners struggle with keeping their plants alive due to a lack of awareness of specific care requirements for different species or misdiagnosed issues. This often leads to frustration, wasted time, and a low satisfaction rate.
projects.goal: Create a solution to reduce diagnosis time and improve user adherence to a plant care schedule.
researchFindings: 24% of Americans admit they cannot keep a houseplant alive for more than six months.
The average plant parent has killed seven indoor plants.
Overwatering, underwatering, neglect, and over-fertilizing are the top four causes of plant death.
One-third of new plant owners struggle with watering.
keyInsights: People often forget to tend to their plants due to busy lifestyles.
Plant owners lack knowledge about species-specific care requirements.
Users do not always have the time or energy to research plant care.
features: Diagnosis and treatment recommendations linked to a storefront

pages.title: To-Do List
pages.description: Track watering, fertilizing, and care tasks

pages.title: Plant Diagnosis
pages.description: Identify diseases and issues through scanning

pages.title: Explore
pages.description: Tips and tricks for plant care

projects.title: WayFinding
projects.subtitle: Library Navigation System
projects.description: A centralized kiosk synced with a mobile app that improves physical library wayfinding through interactive maps and navigation.
projects.problemStatement: Students often struggle to navigate the library, leading to frustration, wasted time, and underutilization of library resources. The library lacks an accessible wayfinding system integrated with its existing database.
projects.goal: Create a solution that improves physical navigation in the library while increasing efficiency and accessibility.
keyInsights: Library anxiety deters people from using available resources.
Users feel uncomfortable repeatedly asking librarians for help.
The library book naming convention is confusing for many users.
Most visitors go directly to the front desk for navigation assistance.
Participants often photograph book numbers on their phones while navigating.

pages.title: Mobile Navigation
pages.description: Guided indoor directions through QR sync

projectPlanning.concept: Users search for books at a centralized kiosk. The kiosk generates a map and QR code that syncs directions to the user’s phone.
technology: Middleware for routing and beacon management

projects.title: ResearchGate
projects.subtitle: Research Opportunity Platform
projects.description: A mobile app that acts as a central hub for researchers to create events and opportunities for students to discover and join.
projects.problemStatement: Students, especially high school students, often lack awareness about research opportunities, creating an information gap between them and the research community. Researchers also struggle to reach younger audiences.
projects.goal: Create a service that spreads awareness about research opportunities and bridges the information gap between students and researchers.
researchFindings: College research creates the foundation for major advances in health, medicine, communication, economics, energy, and national security.
Adolescents ask insightful questions that differ from those of advanced researchers.
Universities can help keep STEM education engaging for younger students.
Only 29% of Gen Z students cite STEM careers as their first choice despite 75% expressing interest in STEM fields.
keyInsights: Researchers are motivated but lack time to organize outreach events.
Researchers see strong potential in highly motivated college students.
AI should act as a supplemental tool rather than replacing human interaction.
Students need greater exposure to research opportunities early on.

metrics.label: Awareness Gap
metrics.description: 73% of surveyed college students reported they were unaware of research opportunities in high school

metrics.label: Stakeholder Interviews
metrics.description: Identified 4 core issues: awareness, resources, platform, and time
